headline: Sarawak Longhouse Electrification Still at 78 Percent
context: Sarawak Energy data shows 22% of longhouse communities totalling 85,000 residents still lack connection to the state electricity grid.

cards.big: 22% of Sarawak longhouses with 85,000 residents still lack electricity
cards.sub: Grid connection in remote highland areas remains economically unfeasible

cards.big: Solar home systems in 3,200 units provide only 4 hours daily power
cards.sub: Systems cost RM8,000 each with 40% failure rate within 3 years

cards.big: Grid extension to remaining communities would cost estimated RM2.4 billion
cards.sub: Cost per connection in remote areas reaches RM120,000 versus RM3,500 urban

cards.big: Micro-hydro projects in 28 Penan villages provide 24-hour power at RM15 million
cards.sub: Model shows community-scale renewables are 8 times cheaper than grid extension

cards.big: Electrification gaps test whether energy access is a right regardless of location cost
78% longhouse electrification after 60 years of statehood. The last 22% is the hardest stretch.
